Module 2:
Tools, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
To continue off a point mentioned in Part 1: There is no one solution
that's going to work for everyone.
These tools just keep on getting better but there's definitely no perfect tool.
Everyone is always looking for a better solution.
Moving to different tools is not a really pleasant thing, but it is doable.
!
Tools, continued:
1. Aweber/Profollow (http://www.sixinseven.com/aweber or http://
profollow.com) - I love Aweber so much that I had them create a private
label for me called Profollow.com. They are now identical services,
there's no difference between them. This is a tool that does one thing
really well - it's where you host your lists. It sends your emails out either
as broadcasts or as autoresponder messages. I've been using it for
more than 10 years, it's a great service.
2. MailChimp (http://mailchimp.com/) - This is a free list host/management
service (similar to Aweber) that a lot of people ask about. I'm not a big
fan of MailChimp, but some people think it's easier to use. They have a
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 3 --

restrictive terms of service that doesn’t allow you to send out affiliate
promotions, so not a good long-term solution.
3. Other list hosts: iContact & Constant Contact - A lot of the alternative
services have less restrictive terms of service agreements compared to
Aweber, in terms of importing existing lists. They can be good if you
have a big list that you've built somewhere and want to import. The
downside is lower deliverability.
4. Ontraport (http://www.sixinseven.com/ontraport) - This is a full all-in-one
solution. It does list management similar to Aweber, but it also has a
shopping cart and affiliate tracking/management built in. It's an
extremely powerful tool. It is much more expensive, but you get a much
more robust feature set and it’s a different type of service.
5. 1ShoppingCart (http://www.sixinseven.com/1sc) - This is similar to, but
not as sophisticated as, Ontraport. It’s got integrated list management, a
shopping cart, and affiliate management. It’s less expensive, much
simpler, but not recommended for list hosting. If I was going to use
1ShoppingCart, I would probably use it in tandem with Aweber.
6. Infustionsoft (http://www.sixinseven.com/infusionsoft) - Very similar to
Ontraport with similar pricing. I've personally never used it, but know a
lot of people that do. I’d recommend comparing both, but these wouldn't
be something you'd want to get when you're first starting out.
7. Synergyx (http://www.sixinseven.com/synergyx) - A standalone
shopping cart that I've used for years. It's not the most sophisticated in
terms of features, but it is a very solid shopping cart. Paul Galloway, the
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 3 --

developer there, provides really good support. Synergyx will also do
your affiliate management for you. I use Synergyx for the shopping cart
and affiliate management, and then Aweber for my email list. This is not
a good “newbie” solution, as it’s not as plug and play as the others.
These are some the tools that I've personally been using for years (and
some others that are in the market) and highly recommended.
To summarize: Aweber, huge fan. Ontraport, big fan. 1ShoppingCart, a
great place to start out. And Infusionsoft, another strong contender there.
Remember: “different horses for different courses”. Don’t overthink this,
just choose what works best for you.
!
